data.title: Sugar Hits Highest Since Mid-May as El Niño Risks Spur Short Covering
data.description: Raw sugar futures climbed to the highest level in more than a month as concerns about El Niño prompted traders to cover short positions, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: A-Share Solar Inverter Stocks Slide July 1; Sungrow Falls Over 13%
data.description:
China’s A-share solar inverter sector weakened on July 1, with Sungrow Power Supply (300274.SZ) dropping nearly 20% intraday and ending down more than 13%, taking its market capitalization below 300 billion yuan, according to Jiemian News. Ginlong Technologies (300763.SZ) closed down 8.67%, while GoodWe (688390.SH), Sineng Electric (300827.SZ), Deye (605117.SH) and Airo Energy (688717.SH) also fell.

Several listed companies told Jiemian News they had heard market rumors about potential overseas market access restrictions for inverter products, but said no policy has been formally implemented and key details such as scope, timeline and enforcement remain unclear. Sungrow said its inverters exported to the US and other markets do not have remote design or wireless communication functions and meet relevant standards. Ginlong said its US business is under 100 million yuan and expects limited direct impact; Deye said the US accounts for about 2%-3% of its sales.

Citing Great Wall Securities data, the report said China’s inverter exports totaled about $943 million in May 2026, up 13.66% year-on-year and 0.63% month-on-month, with export volume at about 4.5719 million units.

data.title: Nissan CEO Espinosa Says 25% US Tariff Hurts Sales of Mexico-Made Cars
data.description:
Nissan Motor Co. President and CEO Ivan Espinosa said US tariffs are making it harder to sell Mexican-made cars in the US, with those vehicles facing a 25% rate, according to Bloomberg.

Espinosa also discussed the future of autonomous driving and Nissan’s investment plans in the US in an interview on “Bloomberg Surveillance.”

data.title: SEC Commissioner Peirce Expects Clarity Act to Pass This Summer After House Approval
data.description:
SEC Commissioner Hester Peirce said she expects the Clarity Act to pass this summer after the bill cleared the House and awaits action in the Senate. According to NS3.AI, the legislation would split oversight of cryptocurrencies between the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC).

Peirce said the bill would establish a federal framework for spot crypto markets.

SEC Chair Paul Atkins said the agency should bring digital asset innovators back under American law.

data.title: Alphabet Shares Fall 15% as Bank of America Maintains Buy Rating and $430 Target
data.description:
Bank of America senior analyst Justin Post reiterated a buy rating on Alphabet’s Google stock and kept a $430 price target after the shares declined. According to NS3.AI, GOOG recently fell 15% to the $345–$350 range.

Post said Alphabet is positioned to benefit from agentic AI systems and large language model layers deployed across its ecosystem.

data.title: Brent Crude Extends Losses 1.6% as U.S. Oil Stock Draw Misses Expectations
data.description:
Brent crude futures extended losses to 1.6% after an EIA report showed U.S. oil inventories fell by less than expected.

According to Jin10, the EIA report indicated that U.S. gasoline futures continued to rise, up 1.1%, as U.S. gasoline inventories declined by more than expected.

data.title: PPP Lists Upcoming Match Schedule for July 2
data.description:
PPP released a list of upcoming matches scheduled for July 2.

According to Odaily, the schedule includes England vs. DR Congo at 00:00 (UTC+8), Belgium vs. Senegal at 04:00 (UTC+8), and the United States vs. Bosnia and Herzegovina at 08:00 (UTC+8).

data.title: STOCKS | Philadelphia Semiconductor Index Falls Over 5% as Micron and Intel Slide
data.description: The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index fell more than 5%. According to Jin10, Micron Technology dropped more than 8%, Intel fell more than 7%, Arm declined 6%, and Nvidia fell 2.6%.

data.title: Bitcoin climbs toward $60,000 after Fed Chair Warsh said inflation risks has come down
data.description: Bitcoin climbed back toward $60,000 after Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh said inflation risks had eased while reaffirming the Fed’s commitment to a 2% inflation target. The token was up more than 2% over the past 24 hours, according to CoinDesk, after Warsh declined to give guidance on the next rate decision ahead of a meeting in four weeks at a panel at the ECB’s annual forum in Sintra, Portugal. Warsh also said AI-driven investment could expand U.S. productive capacity, with potential implications for future monetary policy.

data.title: New York Life Investment Management Launches Tokenized High-Yield Bond Product Settling in USDC
data.description:
New York Life Investment Management, an $807 billion asset manager, has launched a tokenized high-yield corporate bond product with Centrifuge, with subscriptions and redemptions settled in Circle’s USDC.

According to NS3.AI, the product, called HYB, uses USDC for settlement on both subscriptions and redemptions.

The offering documents state that the product is not being offered or sold to U.S. persons.

data.title: MiCA Takes Effect July 1, Leaving Crypto Derivatives Outside Its Scope
data.description:
MiCA fully came into effect today, July 1, while crypto derivatives remain outside its scope. According to NS3.AI, major decentralized perpetual futures exchanges such as Hyperliquid and Aster are not subject to MiCA.

Some licensed crypto companies are raising concerns about reverse discrimination.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Venice AI Raises $65 Million Series A at Over $1 Billion Valuation
data.description:
Venice AI raised $65 million in Series A funding and reached a valuation above $1 billion.

According to NS3.AI, the Swiss-based AI platform plans to use the proceeds to build data centers and secure GPUs.

data.title: Peru’s Inflation Unexpectedly Accelerates as Food Prices Rise
data.description: Inflation in Peru’s capital unexpectedly accelerated in June, remaining above the central bank’s target range for a fourth straight month as food prices rose, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: U.S. EIA Implied Crude Oil Demand Falls to 19.63 Million Barrels a Day in Week Ended June 26
data.description:
U.S. EIA implied crude oil demand fell to 19.63 million barrels per day in the week ended June 26, compared with a previous reading of 20.26 million barrels per day.

According to Jin10, the data reflected EIA figures on implied demand derived from crude oil production for that week.

data.title: U.S. EIA Refinery Utilization Rises to 96.6% in Week Ending June 26
data.description: U.S. EIA refinery utilization was 96.6% in the week ending June 26, compared with an expected 96.3% and a previous reading of 96.1%. According to Jin10, the figures were reported by the U.S. Energy Information Administration.

data.title: EthLabs launches as Ethereum undergoes its biggest leadership transition in years
data.description:
EthLabs has launched as a new nonprofit research organization focused on advancing Ethereum’s technical roadmap with a stronger emphasis on real-world adoption. The group unveiled itself one day before major layoffs at the Ethereum Foundation and days after co-executive director Hsiao-Wei Wang said she would resign, according to CoinDesk, amid broader turnover that has seen at least nine prominent Ethereum Foundation members depart since January.

Executive director Ansgar Dietrichs, a former Ethereum Foundation researcher, said EthLabs is meant to complement—not replace—the foundation by filling adoption-oriented gaps such as scalability, layer-1 performance and interoperability.

data.title: Krafton to Pay ‘Subnautica 2’ Bonuses as Unknown Worlds CEO Ted Gill Resigns
data.description:
Krafton Inc. agreed to pay bonuses tied to ‘Subnautica 2’ after a dispute over a $250 million payout, as Unknown Worlds Entertainment CEO Ted Gill resigned, according to Bloomberg.

Gill had previously been fired and then reinstated amid the payout dispute before leaving the game developer again.

data.title: U.S. EIA Oil, Cushing, and Strategic Petroleum Reserve Inventories Due in 10 Minutes
data.description: U.S. EIA weekly crude oil inventories, Cushing crude oil inventories, and Strategic Petroleum Reserve inventories for the week ending June 26 were due to be released in 10 minutes. According to Jin10, the data release was scheduled imminently.

data.title: AI | Uber Overhauls AI Data-Labeling Unit, Dismissing Two Tech Leaders
data.description: Uber Technologies Inc. dismissed two tech leaders at its nascent AI data-labeling business, shaking up a division the ride-hailing company has positioned as a key growth driver, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Ethereum(ETH) Surpasses 1,600 USDT with a 2.31% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jul 01, 2026, 14:11 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has crossed the 1,600 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 1,604.959961 USDT, with a narrowed 2.31% increase in 24 hours.
